name: Source of the Larráun – circular from Iribas
name_original: Nacedero del Larráun – circular desde Iribas
region_intro: Hiking around Larraun offers diverse landscapes within the Valle de Larraun, characterized by its natural river sources, valleys, and forested areas. The region features a mix of gentle paths and more challenging ascents, with elevations varying across its trails. Specific points of interest include the Nacedero del Larráun and the Ixkier Waterfall. Best hiking trails around Larraun The most popular hiking route is Source of the Larráun – circular from Iribas, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 12 minutes to complete. This easy circular route explores the source of the Larráun river. Another top favourite among local hikers is Iribas loop from Iribas, a moderate 2.8 miles (4.5 km) path. It offers varied terrain around the village of Iribas. Local hikers also love the Millstones of Ixkier – Ixkier Waterfall loop from Pagaditxar, a 4.5 miles (7.3 km) trail leading through natural landscapes to the Ixkier Waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Larraun is defined by river sources, waterfalls, and forested valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels. The routes in Larraun are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Nearly 1,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Larraun's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in the Larraun area?
A: Larraun offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 trails recorded on komoot. These range from easy strolls to more challenging ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Larraun?
A: Yes, Larraun has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e966205129/source-of-the-larraun-circular-from-iribas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Larráun – circular from Iribas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.8-mile route exploring the river's source. There are 6 easy routes in total, often featuring gentle paths through valleys and forests.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Larraun?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Larraun. It's always best to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to follow local regulations. Remember to bring water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Larraun?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Larraun are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Besides the popular 'Source of the Larráun' route, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10522850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millstones of Ixkier – Ixkier Waterfall loop from Pagaditxar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop to the Ixkier Waterfall.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in Larraun?
A: Absolutely! The region is known for its natural river sources and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40317403" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Millstones of Ixkier – Ixkier Waterfall loop from Mugiro&lt;/a&gt; is a great option to experience the beautiful Ixkier Waterfall amidst natural landscapes.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Larraun?
A: Larraun's hiking trails are characterized by diverse natural landscapes, including lush forested areas, serene river valleys, and the sources of rivers like the Larráun. You'll encounter a mix of open spaces and shaded paths, with varying elevations across the region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Larraun?
A: The komoot community highly rates the trails in Larraun, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's varied terrain, the beauty of its river sources and waterfalls, and the well-maintained paths that offer options for all abilities.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or attractions near the hiking trails in Larraun?
A: Yes, the Larraun area is rich in points of interest. You can explore sections of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1118734" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plazaola Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, visit the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1750121" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bartolo Tunnel&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3552575" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aixita Castle Ruins&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4207308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Miguel de Aralar Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable religious site nearby.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in Larraun?
A: Hikes in Larraun vary in duration depending on the trail and your pace. Shorter, easy routes like the 'Source of the Larráun' can take just over an hour, while moderate trails such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15002792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guratz – Añalde loop from Gorriti&lt;/a&gt; can take several hours to complete, offering longer excursions into the landscape.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Larraun?
A: While specific public transport options to every trailhead might be limited, some villages in the Valle de Larraun, which serve as starting points for hikes, may be accessible by local bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for routes connecting to towns like Iribas or Mugiro before planning your trip.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Larraun?
A: Many trailheads in Larraun, particularly those starting from villages like Iribas or Pagaditxar, offer designated parking areas. In smaller, more remote locations, roadside parking might be available. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access or private property.
tips: A beautiful natural place in a very picturesque environment with a variety of trails to get here. This place marks the source of the Iribas river that downstream joins the Ibaia river to become the well-known Larraun.
